reasoning: The market estimated a 69% probability of the weighted average tariff hitting at least 6% in any quarter of 2025 as of March 20. Trump's volatile March actions demonstrate a clear willingness to use executive authority to rapidly enact extreme tariffs (e.g., the March 4 implementation of 25% on Canada/Mexico and 20% on China). Although the Canada/Mexico tariffs were broadly suspended shortly after on March 6, Trump has explicitly scheduled an April 2 deadline for non-compliance and reciprocal tariffs. Because the threshold is just 6% and the baseline is 2.4%, the 20% China tariffs already bring the weighted average significantly closer to 6%. Enacting even a subset of the reciprocal tariffs or resuming the Canada/Mexico tariffs for a notable portion of Q2, Q3, or Q4 would comfortably push the quarterly average above 6%. Given Trump's demonstrated willingness to act combined with the fact he uses tariffs as fluid negotiating tactics, the probability remains high. I estimate it slightly below the late-March market consensus to account for the possibility that he repeatedly suspends broad tariffs before they accumulate enough to shift a full quarter's weighted average past 6%.
